assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) contains 'Verdict: PASS' covering single license filter (criterion-1), comma-separated filter (criterion-2), 400 validation (criterion-3), pagination integration (criterion-4), and unchanged response shape (criterion-5).

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: `modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs`, `modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs` (Files to Modify), and creates `tests/api/package.rs` (Files to Create). No out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Report line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No sub-task creation appears anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are analysis/report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, or other source files) were created or modified in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary of findings, ending with 'This report was generated by the verify-pr skill for task TC-9101.' No merge action, merge command, or merge recommendation appears anywhere in the outputs. The report presents findings for human review only.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No sensitive patterns detected in added lines. The diff contains only Rust source code with no hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, or credentials. All additions are functional code (query parameter parsing, SPDX validation, database filtering) and test code.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in a newly created file (`tests/api/package.rs`). No existing test files were modified or deleted. 4 new test functions added covering the full acceptance criteria.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report summary table contains all nine expected verdict rows: Scope Containment (line 7, PASS), Diff Size (line 8, PASS), Commit Traceability (line 9, PASS) — Intent Alignment domain; Sensitive Patterns (line 10, PASS) — Security domain; CI Status (line 11, PASS), Acceptance Criteria (line 12, PASS), Verification Commands (line 15, N/A) — Correctness domain; Test Quality (line 13, PASS), Test Change Classification (line 14, ADDITIVE) — Style/Conventions domain. All expected verdicts from all four domains are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: The report has detailed per-criterion findings in separate files (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) covering the Correctness domain with code-level verification. However, for Intent Alignment, Security, and Style/Conventions domains, the findings are confined to brief text within summary table cells (e.g., Scope Containment lists files in one sentence on line 7, Sensitive Patterns is a single sentence on line 10, Test Quality is a single sentence on line 13). The assertion explicitly requires detailed findings 'not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table' — the Intent Alignment, Security, and Style/Conventions evidence is entirely within the summary table, without separate detailed sections showing file-by-file scope comparison, line-level pattern scanning results, or test quality assessment.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These two verdicts appear at the top of the table (lines 5-6), positioned before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), with independent reasoning that does not reference domain analysis results.
